speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Will not King Richard let me speak with him?

speaker: SHERIFF
text: No, my good lord. Therefore be patient.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Hastings and Edward’s children, Grey and Rivers,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Holy King Henry and thy fair son Edward,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Vaughan, and all that have miscarrièd

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: By underhand, corrupted, foul injustice,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: If that your moody, discontented souls

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Do through the clouds behold this present hour,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Even for revenge mock my destruction.—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This is All Souls’ Day, fellow, is it not?

speaker: SHERIFF
text: It is.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Why, then, All Souls’ Day is my body’s doomsday.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This is the day which, in King Edward’s time,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I wished might fall on me when I was found

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: False to his children and his wife’s allies.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This is the day wherein I wished to fall

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: By the false faith of him whom most I trusted.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This, this All Souls’ Day to my fearful soul

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Is the determined respite of my wrongs.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: That high All-seer which I dallied with

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Hath turned my feignèd prayer on my head

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And given in earnest what I begged in jest.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Thus doth he force the swords of wicked men

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To turn their own points in their masters’ bosoms.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Thus Margaret’s curse falls heavy on my neck:

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: “When he,” quoth she, “shall split thy heart with sorrow,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Remember Margaret was a prophetess.”—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Come, lead me, officers, to the block of shame.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Wrong hath but wrong, and blame the due of blame.
